breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK:
text:
Carry out insulation jobs on all equipment and piping including valves, flanges etc (Scope
as mentioned In Annexure 1 – Lumpsum scope of jobs”. in units during T&I.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 1 SCOPE OF WORK:
text: 1 SCOPE OF WORK:

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 1 SCOPE OF WORK:
text:
a. Removal of damaged thermal insulation (and insulation where inspection of
pipeline is required) along with external cladding and aluminum foil as per
instructions of Job Engineer. While removing insulation, the cladding that is
in good condition and electrical tracing shall not be damaged.
b. Segregation of all reusable insulation material for reuse and transfer all
damaged material to an area as directed by Job Engineer.
c. Clean the piping/ equipment at areas where the insulation was removed &
offer for Inspection.
d. After the inspection, repairs and testing of the piping /equipment, Agency to
supply (new Insulation material) & has to fix back the Insulation along with
cladding.
e. Agency to erect scaffolding for insulation removal if required.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 2 Removal of Insulation:
text: 2 Removal of Insulation:

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 2 Removal of Insulation:
text:
a. Remove carefully the thermal insulation and cladding as per instructions to
expose/clean the piping/ equipment, wire brush, scrap with scrappers to St
2 standard for inspection and transfer material to an area as directed by Job
Engineer.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 3 Cleaning & Painting:
text: 3 Cleaning & Painting:

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 3 Cleaning & Painting:
text:
a. After inspection of the line, manually clean the surface to be insulated with
wire brush free of all scales, rust, oil etc. to St 2.
b. Carry out painting of the surfaces under insulation using two coats heat
resistant aluminum paint of 15 microns DFT/coat and capable of
withstanding 200 deg C.
c. For those lines that are in service and hot where painting is not possible
wrap aluminum foil of 0.18 mm thick around the pipe with an overlap of 50
mm longitudinally and circumferentially and secure with 18 SWG GI wire
with 220 mm spacing.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 4 Fixing of LRB mattresses:
text: 4 Fixing of LRB mattresses:

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 4 Fixing of LRB mattresses:
text:
a. Refix old LRB mattresses using new 1/2” wire x 24 SWG GI hexagonal wire
netting on one face and secure the mattresses in position with 24 SWG GI
wire lace and new 18 SWG GI binding wire with 220 mm spacing.
b. Supply and apply new LRB (light resin bonded) mineral wool mattresses of
density 150 kg/cum with 1/2” wire x 24 SWG GI hexagonal wire netting on

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 4 Fixing of LRB mattresses:
text: one face and secure the mattresses in position with 24 SWG GI wire lace and 18 SWG GI wire (12 SWG wire for vessels /piping above 16” dia) with 220 mm spacing. c. Refix old calcium silicate blocks /pipe sections using 18 SWG SS 304 wire 220 mm pitch (using 20 mm wide 24 SWG SS 304 bands at 220 mm centres above 16” dia). Fix the ends of the wires into the blocks after tightening. Fill the gaps at the seams with calcium silicate paste and sodium silicate solution. d. Apply new ceramic fiber supplied by HPCL to the piping/ducts and equipment using 19 SWG SS 304 wire (12 SWG SS 304 wire for vessels/ piping above 16” dia ) with 200 mm spacing. e. Supply and apply ceramic fiber to the piping and equipment using 19 SWG SS 304 wire (12 SWG wire for vessels/ piping above 16” dia ) with 200 mm spacing. 5. Cladding a. Supply and apply new cladding of 22 SWG plain aluminum sheet coated on one side in contact with insulation with anticorrosive bitumen paint, over insulated surface with the longitudinal seams staggered. b. Fix in position using No.8 dia x 13 mm long self tapping GI screws with neoprene washers under the head at 150 mm centers on the longitudinal joints. c. For corrugated sheets cladding to cladding fastening at both horizontal and vertical overlaps shall be alternately by pop rivets and

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 4 Fixing of LRB mattresses:
text: long self tapping GI screws with neoprene washers under the head at 150 mm centers on the longitudinal joints. c. For corrugated sheets cladding to cladding fastening at both horizontal and vertical overlaps shall be alternately by pop rivets and self tapping screws at 150 mm pitch. The edge of the cladding sheets shall be grooved for a perfect fit up with an overlap of 50 mm at the seams longitudinally and circumferentially. The cladding joints shall be sealed using butyl mastic tape / tar felt with minimum thickness of 3 mm and width of 25 mm. 6. Rope Lagging a. For small bore pipes upto 1 ½” diameter, supply & wrap ½” diameter asbestos rope spirally around the pipe without any gap between rope to rope and secure with 18 SWG GI wire with 220 mm spacing. b. Provide 1/2” thick layer of hard setting plaster of cement and asbestos and protect with one thick coat of shalikote or bituminous compound. 7. Mastic Application a. Supply and apply 3 mm thick coating of mastic on LRB and whilst the coating is wet, lay glass cloth over the surface and embedded in mastic without wrinkles with minimum overlap of 75 mm at joints.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 4 Fixing of LRB mattresses:
text: b. Apply a second coat of 3 mm thick mastic after approximately 12 hours. The dry coat shall be about 1.5 mm thick. 8. Spacer Rings a. Supply and fix spacer rings fabricated out of 25 mm x 3 mm MS flats. The outside diameter of these rings shall be equivalent to outside diameter of insulation. Spacer rings shall be riveted to ‘Z’ shaped stays fabricated from the same sized flat using 5 mm MS rivets. b. The ‘Z’ shaped stays shall be spaced at intervals of not more than 300 mm along the circumference of the piping and subject to minimum of 3 stays. Spacer rings shall be provided at intervals of approx. 900 mm along the pipe. Joints between MS spacer rings shall be made using 6 mm id. c. MS bolts with two sheets of 3 mm thick asbestos mill board interposed. 9. Cement Plaster a. Apply 12 mm thick cement plaster 1:4 ( 1 cement : 4 Sand ) with coarse and and 42 grade OP cement on LRB insulated surface on pipelines with ¾” x 24 G GI mesh sandwiched in cement mortar neatly finished and curing with water for specified period 10. SS bands a. Supply and fixing in position SS 304 bands on corrugated sheets on tanks, vessels tightened, locked and lock fastened featuring SS fastening system. In order to prevent sliding of the bands downwards, the bands shall be secured to the

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 4 Fixing of LRB mattresses:
text: a. Supply and fixing in position SS 304 bands on corrugated sheets on tanks, vessels tightened, locked and lock fastened featuring SS fastening system. In order to prevent sliding of the bands downwards, the bands shall be secured to the cladding using pop rivets at horizontal pitch of not over 2 meters. b. 20 mm width SS bands shall be used on piping and 25 mm width on tanks . c. Aluminium bands shall be of 13 mm width for piping at 500 mm centers and 20 mm width for vessels at 1000 mm centers. 11. Insulation Pockets a. Provide approx. 2” to 8” inspection pockets on the equipment piping for the purpose of inspection by cutting pockets on existing cladding; remove the mineral wool / lagging and clean the exposed surface in the pocket for inspection. b. After completion of inspection, provide new clad overlay for the inspection pockets after filling the gap with mineral wool and fixing in position using No 8 dia x 13 mm long self tapping GI screws and sealing the clad joints using elastomeric sealing compound.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: 12 GENERAL NOTES:

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: a. While applying multi-layer lagging, all joints shall be staggered & each layer shall be separately secured to the surface. b. Fittings on all vessels shall be covered with an independent insulation so as to allow easy access and removal without disturbing the main insulation. The word “fittings” shall include elbows, reducers, tees, vessels, flanges, bends, end caps, expansion/contraction joints, venturi, orifice plates, etc. c. Sign writing of product details on the insulated portions and heat tracing junction box details on the cladding shall be carried out by the Agency on all areas insulated by them. Payments for the sign writing / stenciling shall be separate and shall be as per Schedule of Rates. d. Where old insulation materials/ cladding is being reused the Agency has to use new holding mesh/ binding wire and screws at no extra cost to HPCL. e. Material supply and test certificates: i. The bonded mineral wool shall be as per IS:8183: 1993 and of density 150 kg/m 3 and shall be from M/s Lloyds Insulation India Private Limited or M/s Minwool Rock Fibre or M/s Rockwool India Limited or M/s Goenka Rockwool ( India) Private Limited or any other manufacturer approved by EIl, SMMS. Slag wool is not acceptable. The minimum acceptable density of the bonded mineral wool shall be 140 kg / cub m and the average shall be 150 kg/ cub m . Thickness of LRB mineral wool layer shall not exceed

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: Slag wool is not acceptable. The minimum acceptable density of the bonded mineral wool shall be 140 kg / cub m and the average shall be 150 kg/ cub m . Thickness of LRB mineral wool layer shall not exceed 75 mm. ii. The calcium silicate blocks shall be as per IS 8154-1993 with density of 250 +/- 25 kg/m 3 and shall be round sections for pipe sizes 2” upto 12” OD and curved sections above 12”; agency shall have the option of supplying QADS - 4 pieces per circle for pipe sizes 10” to 14”. Calcium silicate blocks from M/s Hyderabad Industries Limited or M/s Newkem , Bombay shall be used. For multi layer minimum thickness of each layer shall not be less than 50 mm. Length of CSB pipe section, segment, slab supplied shall not be less than 900 mm. iii. Ceramic fibre shall be of Grade 1260 deg C and as per ASTM C892 and with chloride content less than 10 ppm. iv. For rope insulation lagging rope shall be of Champion make or equivalent approved make. v. The aluminum sheet and foil shall be as per IS:737-1986. vi. Tar felt shall be as per IS 1322 - Type 3 , Grade 1. vii. The pre- painted sheet shall be as per IS 14246 . The GI sheet shall be as per IS 277 with minimum zinc coating of 180 grams/ sq m and painted on

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: 1322 - Type 3 , Grade 1. vii. The pre- painted sheet shall be as per IS 14246 . The GI sheet shall be as per IS 277 with minimum zinc coating of 180 grams/ sq m and painted on the inside with 4 to 6 microns epoxy coating and on the outside (top exposed side) with 4 to 6 microns of epoxy primer and with 16 to 20 microns of polyester top coat (off white colour) on the primer. viii. Heat resistant aluminum paint shall be capable of withstanding 200 deg C (to IS2339) and from M/s Asian Paints, Akzo Nobel, Berger

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: Paints, Coromandel Paints, Jenson & Nicholson, Shalimar Paints or Sigma Coatings or any other paint manufacturer approved by HPCL. f. Agency shall submit manufacturers test certificates with all relevant details regarding the insulation and other materials being used and shall take HPCL approval at every stage of the job which shall be submitted along with the bill. In case of any doubt HPCL shall advise the agency to carry out tests at specified laboratories at vendor’s cost. Agency shall replace non-confirming material at their cost. Wet or damaged insulation shall not be used. g. All materials brought inside Refinery to be declared at the gate and copies of gate entries submitted to the Job Engineer for material reconciliation. Metal scrap generated during fabrication of the aluminium /clad sheets shall not be taken out of the refinery and shall be handed over to HPCL. Only the intact and unused sheets/coil, left over if any after successful completion of the job by the Agency can be taken out of the refinery after material reconciliation. h. All measurements shall be as per IS:14164:2008 and as following: For pipe lines/equipment without steam tracers, area for the LRB mineral wool and cladding will be arrived based on nominal OD of the piping/equipment and nominal thickness of the insulation. For bends/tee joints extra measurement will be allowed for cladding and calcium silicate insulation and mineral wool lagging. In case intervening valve/fitting in a pipe line is

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: based on nominal OD of the piping/equipment and nominal thickness of the insulation. For bends/tee joints extra measurement will be allowed for cladding and calcium silicate insulation and mineral wool lagging. In case intervening valve/fitting in a pipe line is not insulated, measurement in such cases shall be done up to the nearest flange. However these intervening valves will be included if the ends of the insulation on either side of the Valve / Fitting Flange are finished with end caps; for sectional replacement of insulation (for intermediate sections of piping / equipment), actual replacement sections will be considered for payment for lagging and cladding. For lagging thickness above 150 mm thick payment shall be made basis 150 mm thick lagging extrapolated. i. Stage wise inspection shall be offered. For replacement of lagging and cladding written instructions from the Job Engineer is to be taken. Measurements on all new and old lagging shall be certified by Job Engineer prior to fixing of clad sheet. After each layer of lagging completion job Engineer clearance is to be taken with dates. No claim can be made for lagging after completion of clad works. j. Inspection of the works shall be by HPCL/ HPCL representative k. Agency shall give a guarantee of one year for the insulation and painting works. l. Whenever insulation has to be removed at pipe supports for the purpose of Inspection, minimum portion of the insulation shall be removed.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: HPCL representative k. Agency shall give a guarantee of one year for the insulation and painting works. l. Whenever insulation has to be removed at pipe supports for the purpose of Inspection, minimum portion of the insulation shall be removed. After Inspection it shall be covered with independent insulation so as to permit ease of removal of insulation without disturbing the main insulation. m. Agency shall make own arrangements for working and storing limited quantity of insulation at open space specified by HPCL

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: 13. Housekeeping a. The work site shall be maintained always in clean and safe condition. b. Housekeeping shall be carried out on a daily basis at each location after completion of the job. c. The loose insulation & cladding shall be removed from site immediately after removal and shifted to a temporary storage area within the unit or designated location advised by HPCL. Loose insulation shall be kept in large bags (in agency scope). d. Aluminium Cladding Sheet Scrap Disposal i. All removed unusable lagging/ clad sheets shall be transported to a designated place in the Refinery. ii. The clad sheets are to be initially transported without weighment slips to location indicated by Job Engineer. iii. After the quantity of clad sheets is sizeable, the sheets are to be segregated as GI/ colour sheets/ aluminium; compacted, weighed and transported to ATP store yard (in HPCL premises) area for final disposal. iv. The scrap weighment slips shall be submitted to HPCL Maintenance TPD for records v. In case, the Agency fails to maintain good house keeping, HPCL shall offload the house- keeping job to any other Agency and charges as certified by HPCL Job Engineer shall be deducted from the Agency’s account e. Disposal of Non-Oil Soaked Insulation i. The agency shall collect, load, transport and dispose off dry non-oil soaked used insulation material waste from the unit out of Refinery premises. ii. This includes insulation waste material removed from any

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text: account e. Disposal of Non-Oil Soaked Insulation i. The agency shall collect, load, transport and dispose off dry non-oil soaked used insulation material waste from the unit out of Refinery premises. ii. This includes insulation waste material removed from any equipment, piping etc during the shutdown of the unit. iii. Disposal to be done to location earmarked for the disposal purpose by the statutory / municipal / environmental authorities concerned, at no extra cost to HPCL. The identification of such disposal areas outside HPCL-Visakh Refinery premises shall be the responsibility of the agency. It shall also be the sole responsibility of the agency to obtain all necessary approvals from the authorities concerned iv. The insulation material waste shall be transported and disposed off by the agency on “As is Where is” basis v. The loose insulation material shall be packed in gunny / plastic bags of approximately 1M x 1M x 1M size (to be arranged by the agency) and packed tight and the opening shall be sealed by stitching / tying using metallic / plastic wires.

breadcrumb: 1. SCOPE OF WORK: > 12 GENERAL NOTES:
text:
vi. HPCL shall provide necessary documents to the agency for obtaining
such approvals and all other coordination work shall be arranged by
agency
vii. The agency shall indemnify HPCL once the agency takes the
insulation waste material out of the Refinery premises.
viii. It shall be the sole responsibility of the agency to comply with all the
environmental & statutory rules & regulations prevailing at the time
of disposal of non-oil soaked used insulation material being taken out
of HPCL, Visakh Refinery
ix. Only dry non-oil soaked used insulation material waste as certified by
the job engineer of HPCL shall be taken out and no other insulation
materials including oil soaked insulation material waste shall be taken
out of Refinery.
x. The agency shall make necessary arrangements to mobilise trucks /
tractors for collecting used / waste insulation from the designated
locations.
f. Disposal of Oil Soaked Insulation:
i. To be properly packed and disposed at designated place within
refinery as directed by the HPCL job engineer.
